text.title: competitor-analysis Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for competitor-analysis as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Analysing only direct competitors and omitting indirect and future types.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Indirect competitors are what customers actually use today; future competitors are the real threat in 18 months. Missing both produces a false sense of whitespace.

failure-mode.description: Surface-level analysis limited to homepage copy without digging into pricing pages, reviews, and feature documentation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pricing footnotes (annual lock-in, seat minimums, transaction fees) and 1-star review themes contain more actionable signal than marketing copy.

failure-mode.description: One-time analysis with no refresh schedule.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A competitor matrix is stale within 60-90 days. Pricing, feature flags, and team size change frequently. Schedule quarterly re-runs with the same template to produce a diff.

failure-mode.description: Copying competitors' features rather than differentiating on their weaknesses.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Feature parity produces table stakes, not a positioning story. Your differentiation is their weakness as expressed in customer complaints.

failure-mode.description: Accepting funding figures, employee counts, or revenue estimates without a verified source.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without Crunchbase API access, agents invent plausible numbers. Mitigation: hard rule "if Crunchbase API unavailable, write 'unknown'—never estimate". Mark every unverified number with "(est.)".

failure-mode.description: Accepting a pricing number without checking effective monthly cost for 1 user billed monthly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Shown price excludes annual lock-in, seat minimums, and transaction fees. Always ask "what is the effective monthly price for 1 user, billed monthly?" before accepting any pricing cell.

failure-mode.description: Trusting G2 or Capterra star ratings as objective quality signals.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Paid placement skews stars upward; incentivised reviews under-report weaknesses. Cross-check with Reddit and Hacker News sentiment before quoting any rating.

failure-mode.description: Listing 5+ future competitors with no concrete signal (job posting, acquisition, beta product).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents over-list "Google could enter here" type future competitors. Cap at 2-3 and require one concrete signal per future competitor or it inflates the threat map.

failure-mode.description: Dumping raw scrapes from 10+ competitor websites into a single synthesis prompt.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Context overflows degrade gap analysis quality. Pre-summarise each competitor to the snapshot template (one Task call per competitor), then synthesise the summaries in a final pass.
